mapping.a_element: Bayesian updating: prior over attractor-convergence probabilities updated with newly sampled data to a posterior
mapping.b_element: Bayesian inference: prior over graviton mass updated with PTA angular-correlation data to a posterior

mapping.a_element: Dirichlet-multinomial likelihood for which attractor a sampled initial condition reaches
mapping.b_element: likelihood of the measured tensor angular-correlation given the modified dispersion relation

mapping.a_element: log Bayes factor used to compare the historical prior against structural change
mapping.b_element: comparison across noise configurations / inclusion of monopolar-dipolar terms

brief_justification: The only genuine overlap is the generic use of Bayesian inference (prior + likelihood -> posterior); the transferable machinery differs. Paper A's distinctive method is adaptive continuation that exploits the persistence of basin boundaries in multistable dynamical systems, using a Dirichlet-multinomial model and a log-Bayes-factor sensor to trigger dense resampling at bifurcations, whereas Paper B is a static Bayesian parameter-constraint pipeline that sets credible upper limits on a graviton mass from a modified dispersion relation and PTA correlation data — there is no counterpart to A's basin-tracking / adaptive-resampling mechanism.
